sources.title: 20-month field tests show tracker-based bifacial PV outpeforms fixed tilt counterparts with 13.5% higher ouput in desert enviroments
sources.excerpt: Qatari researchers found that bifacial 2P horizontal single-axis tracking PV systems in desert conditions produced up to 13.5% more electricity than fixed-tilt systems over long-term field testing in Qatar. title: Solar Manufacturing USA 2026 – production and technology at the heart of US solar

sources.title: Solar Manufacturing USA 2026 – production and technology at the heart of US solar
sources.excerpt: The upcoming Solar Manufacturing USA 2026 conference in Austin will focus on real U.S. solar manufacturing progress, shifting attention from capacity announcements to actual production, costs, yields, and technology choices across the full value chain. title: Scientists discover why lead-halide solar perovskites work efficiently despite being structurally ‘messy’
summary: Researchers in Austria found that unusually high photovoltaic efficiency in lead-halide perovskites can be explained by internal electric fields generated at strain-induced domain walls, which separat

sources.title: Scientists discover why lead-halide solar perovskites work efficiently despite being structurally ‘messy’
sources.excerpt: Researchers in Austria found that unusually high photovoltaic efficiency in lead-halide perovskites can be explained by internal electric fields generated at strain-induced domain walls, which separate charge carriers and enable long-range transport. Their experiments indicated that these effects arise from mesoscopic structural inhomogeneities rather than a uniform crystal structure, providing a general mechanism for charge separation in these materials.
